meta.maintenanceInstruction: KEEP THE PLAN UP TO DATE AS YOU EVOLVE THE CODE. DEVIATIONS TO THE PLAN MUST BE LOGGED WITHIN THE PLAN. THE PLAN MUST BE REASSESSED HOLISTICALLY AFTER ANY CHANGE OF ANY SIZE.
meta.completionPolicy: YOU ARE NOT DONE UNTIL YOU HAVE COMPLETED 100% OF THE BLUEPRINT. DO NOT STOP. YOU MUST REFINE AT REGULAR INTERVALS TO KEEP THE BLUEPRINT FULL. NEVER SPECIFY ANY ESTIMATE OF EFFORT OR COST — ESPECIALLY NOT TIME ESTIMATES. NEVER SPECIFY A PRIORITY OF ANY KIND.
sourceDocuments: scratch/bubbletea-v2-migration.md — Original v2-first migration plan (7 phases, zero shims mandate)
scratch/migration-guide.md — Official Bubble Tea v2 upgrade guide
scratch/termmux-architecture.md — SessionManager architecture and implementation notes
scratch/textarea-navigation-autopsy-20260412/ — Adversarial autopsy of textarea key handling (CORRECTED: see replanLog)

globalAlerts.warning: ALL checks must pass on ALL platforms (ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest). Never accept failing or flaky tests.
GNU Make is the build system. Use make test, make lint, make build. On macOS use gmake.
ZERO COMPATIBILITY SHIMS: v2 ONLY. No v1 mouse patterns (isWheel, action, shift/alt/ctrl booleans). v2 uses type-based dispatch and msg.mod array.
BRIDGE SYMMETRY: msgToJS and JsToTeaMsg MUST agree on event shapes. Asymmetric bridges are defective.

KEY RESOLUTION DEFENSIVE FIX: The JS key resolution at super_document_script.js:870 currently works because bubbletea v2 sets Key.Text='' (empty) for special keys (Enter, Tab, Esc, arrows) — confirmed by ultraviolet decoder.go:1139-1144 and bubbletea key.go:297-300. The existing `msg.text && msg.text.length === 1` check is safe for current runtime behavior but fragile against future Key.Text population changes. Apply defensive hardening: `const k = (msg.text && msg.text.length === 1 && msg.text.charCodeAt(0) >= 0x20 && msg.text.charCodeAt(0) <= 0x7E) ? msg.text : msg.key;`.

statusSection.currentState: CRITICAL BUG FIXED: keys_gen.go rune corruption. Ultraviolet uses rune values above U+10FFFF (e.g., KeyUp=0x110001) which Go's %q format silently truncates to U+FFFD (replacement character). Regenerated keys_gen.go using rune(0xHHHHHH) format. All arrow/navigation keys now work. E2E textarea test passes. Tasks 1-3 complete, awaiting Rule of Two and commit.
statusSection.goalState: Production-ready merged codebase: zero v1 patterns, all textarea keys verified working at runtime via integration tests, all tests green on 3 platforms, complete documentation, then STUB tasks and future features expanding towards True Perfection.
replanLog: 2026-04-12: AUTOPSY INTEGRATION + RULE OF TWO CORRECTION. (1) Removed 6 Done tasks from prior merge work. (2) Integrated autopsy findings from scratch/textarea-navigation-autopsy-20260412/. (3) CRITICAL CORRECTION during Rule of Two Pass 2: the autopsy's core claim that Enter/Tab are broken was based on a static analysis error — it confused Key.Code (rune '\r'/'\t') with Key.Text (string field, which is '' for special keys per ultraviolet decoder.go:1139-1144 and bubbletea key.go:297-300). The key pipeline is structurally correct for ALL keys. (4) Refocused tasks: defensive hardening of msg.text resolution (not a bug fix but a safety guard), comprehensive runtime verification tests, cross-platform, documentation, diff audit. (5) Added stub tasks per DIRECTIVE.txt for future scope. (6) Autopsy value: mapped entire key pipeline end-to-end, confirmed arrow keys structurally reach textarea, identified PgUp/PgDown as intentionally intercepted by scroll handler.

2026-04-12: CRITICAL BUG FOUND AND FIXED via E2E TDD. The E2E textarea test (task 3) exposed a catastrophic bug: arrow/navigation keys were silently eaten by the textarea. Root cause: keys_gen.go code generator used fmt.Sprintf %q for rune values, but ultraviolet's key constants (KeyUp=0x110001, KeyDown=0x110002, etc.) are ABOVE the valid Unicode range (U+10FFFF). Go's %q format silently replaces these with U+FFFD (replacement character). ParseKey('up') returned Code=0xFFFD instead of Code=0x110001, causing Key.String() to return '�' instead of 'up', which doesn't match the textarea's key.Matches bindings. Fix: generator now uses formatRuneLiteral() — rune(0xHHHHHH) for out-of-range values, strconv.QuoteRune for valid Unicode. Affected keys: ALL navigation keys (up/down/left/right/home/end/pgup/pgdown/delete/insert/begin/select/find + F-keys + keypad + shift/ctrl variants). Non-affected: Enter, Tab, Backspace, Esc, Space, Ctrl+letter (all within ASCII range). This bug predated the v2 migration and affected ALL textarea navigation since keys_gen.go was first generated.

mandatoryDirectives.contextManagement: Each task covers one cohesive change. Read relevant source files before each task.
mandatoryDirectives.shimProhibition: If any task introduces or preserves a v1 compatibility shim, STOP and replan.
mandatoryDirectives.noShimPeriods: There is NO transitional period where both v1 and v2 behaviors coexist.
mandatoryDirectives.adversarialVerification: REGULARLY spawn fresh subagents instructed to read Skill adversarial-codebase-autopsy and exhaustively verify work believed to be Done. The executing agent has repeatedly proven lazy and inept. Do not trust self-assessment.
mandatoryDirectives.tddMandate: For ALL bug fixes and behavioral changes: (1) Write a failing test FIRST that reproduces the bug or validates the expected behavior. (2) Verify the test FAILS before applying the fix. (3) Apply the fix. (4) Verify the test PASSES. (5) Verify no other relevant tests regress. For improvements and refactors: run the FULL relevant test suite (not just -short) before AND after the change.
mandatoryDirectives.testCoverage: Do NOT rely on -short tests alone. Many JS integration tests (especially super-document, TUI, REPL) are SKIPPED by -short. Run full test suites for the relevant packages when the change touches JS or integration code.

sequentialTasks.task: Harden msg.text resolution: add printable-filter guard at super_document_script.js:870
sequentialTasks.description: In `internal/command/super_document_script.js` line 870, the key resolution `const k = msg.text && msg.text.length === 1 ? msg.text : msg.key;` works correctly at runtime because bubbletea v2 sets `Key.Text = ''` (empty) for special keys — `msg.text` is falsy so `k` correctly falls through to `msg.key`. HOWEVER, this is a fragile assumption: if bubbletea ever populates `Key.Text` for control characters (e.g., `Key.Text = '\r'` for Enter), the length-1 check would resolve `k` to that control character instead of the named key. Apply defensive hardening: change to `const k = (msg.text && msg.text.length === 1 && msg.text.charCodeAt(0) >= 0x20 && msg.text.charCodeAt(0) <= 0x7E) ? msg.text : msg.key;`. This guarantees only printable ASCII (0x20-0x7E) uses msg.text; everything else uses msg.key. Also update the comment at lines 866-869 to document WHY this filter exists (defensive hardening against future Key.Text changes) and the verified behavior (Key.Text is empty for special keys per ultraviolet decoder and bubbletea docs). References: ultraviolet decoder.go:1139-1144, bubbletea key.go:297-300, autopsy 03_gap_analysis.md GAP-001.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: The JS key resolution line explicitly filters for printable ASCII (0x20-0x7E) before using msg.text. Comment documents the defensive rationale. Existing behavior is unchanged: all keys (arrows, Enter, Tab, Space, printable chars, backspace, delete, home, end) continue to work correctly. Verified by the integration tests in the next task.
sequentialTasks.completedNote: Applied printable-ASCII guard (0x20-0x7E) at super_document_script.js handleKeys(). Comment documents defensive rationale with references to ultraviolet decoder and bubbletea docs.

sequentialTasks.task: Write comprehensive runtime verification tests for textarea key handling
sequentialTasks.description: Create integration tests that verify the full JS→Go bridge for textarea keys at runtime. Test file: `internal/command/super_document_test.go` or a new dedicated test file. Cover ALL keys from the autopsy evidence chain (02_evidence_chain.md lines 153-169): ↑, ↓, ←, →, Enter, Tab, Space, Backspace, Esc, Home, End, PgUp, PgDn, Delete, plus printable letters (a, Z, 5). For each key: (1) Verify msgToJS produces the correct JS object — specifically verify `msg.text` is empty for special keys and populated only for printable chars. (2) Verify the JS k resolution produces the correct k value. (3) Verify ValidateTextareaInput(k) returns expected result. (4) Verify the key reaches (or is correctly intercepted before) textarea.update(). Use existing test patterns from `internal/builtin/bubbletea/input_validation_test.go` and `coverage_gaps_test.go`. Tests must use skipSlow(t) if spawning JS runtimes (per AGENTS.md). Also add a specific test that verifies Key.Text is empty for Enter and Tab by directly checking msgToJS output — this serves as a regression guard against the autopsy's incorrect assumption.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A test suite covering all 14+ keys from the evidence chain table exists and passes. Each key has a test verifying the full pipeline. A dedicated test asserts that msgToJS produces `text: ''` for Enter and Tab (regression guard against the autopsy's mistaken claim). PgUp/PgDown tests verify they are intercepted by the scroll handler. Esc test verifies it is intercepted by the esc handler. All tests pass with `go test -v ./internal/command/... -run TestTextareaKey` and existing `go test -v ./internal/builtin/bubbletea/... -run TestValidateTextareaInput` continues to pass.
sequentialTasks.completedNote: 4 test functions in internal/builtin/bubbletea/key_pipeline_test.go: TestKeyPipeline_FullChain (18 subtests covering Enter/Tab/Esc/Backspace/Delete/arrows/Home/End/PgUp/PgDown/Space/a/Z/5/Ctrl+C through msgToJS→resolveKeyJS→ValidateTextareaInput), TestKeyPipeline_SpecialKeysHaveEmptyText (13 special keys regression guard), TestKeyPipeline_PrintableGuardRejectsControlChars (6 control chars), TestKeyPipeline_SpaceUsesTextNotNamedKey. All 37 subtests pass.

sequentialTasks.task: E2E runtime verification: arrow keys and Enter produce visible textarea behavior
sequentialTasks.description: Write an E2E test that verifies textarea behavior at the terminal level, addressing UNCERTAIN items 6-8 from autopsy 04_honest_conclusions.md. Test in `internal/command/super_document_e2e_test.go` or `internal/scripting/super_document_unix_integration_test.go` that: (1) opens the super-document TUI, (2) enters MODE_INPUT, (3) focuses the content textarea (FOCUS_CONTENT), (4) types multi-line content including Enter for newlines, (5) presses arrow keys and verifies cursor position changes, (6) verifies the view output reflects the changes. Use existing E2E infrastructure (`internal/mouseharness/`, VT parser with WaitForContent/WaitForScreenContent). This test is the definitive proof that the key pipeline works end-to-end, not just structurally. If issues are found, document them in WIP.md with the specific failure mode and add fix tasks before the cross-platform tasks.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: An E2E test exists that types multi-line content into the textarea (proving Enter works for newlines), then presses arrow keys and verifies visible cursor movement. The test passes on macOS. This test proves the full pipeline from terminal→bubbletea→JS→textarea→view works for the key types the autopsy investigated.
sequentialTasks.completedNote:
TDD pattern: (1) Wrote TestSuperDocument_TextareaArrowKeysAndEnter in super_document_unix_integration_test.go. (2) Test FAILED exposing real bug: arrows silently eaten, output showed 'AAAA BBBBX' instead of 'XAAAA
BBBB'. (3) Added TestKeyPipeline_ParseKeyRoundTrip which diagnosed root cause: KeyDefs['up'].Code=0xFFFD (replacement char) instead of 0x110001 (tea.KeyUp). (4) Fixed generator (formatRuneLiteral for out-of-range runes), regenerated keys_gen.go. (5) Round-trip test PASSES, E2E test PASSES (1.44s). Critical fix: all navigation keys were broken since keys_gen.go creation.

sequentialTasks.description: Run `make make-all-in-container` (see config.mk). All tests must pass including the new textarea key tests. The container runs the full `make all` inside `golang:<version>` from go.mod.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: `make make-all-in-container` exits 0. All 43+ packages pass. Zero test failures on Linux.
sequentialTasks.completedNote: 6 Docker runs. 3 Linux-only flakes fixed (REPLTUIToggle cooked-mode race, Chunk10b 0-iteration timeout, StressBoundedMemoryGrowth poll loop). Committed as 24965d86. Rule of Two passed (2/2 PASS).

sequentialTasks.description: Run `make make-all-run-windows` (see config.mk). Uses `hack/run-on-windows.sh` to execute `make all` on Windows. All tests must pass.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: `make make-all-run-windows` exits 0. All packages pass on Windows.

sequentialTasks.task: Documentation sweep: verify accuracy for v2 API and key handling
sequentialTasks.description: Verify all files under `docs/` are accurate for the current v2 API. Specifically: (1) No references to v1 mouse patterns (isWheel, action, shift/alt/ctrl booleans); (2) No references to deleted types or pre-merge APIs; (3) `docs/scripting.md` documents the key event handling pattern including msg.text resolution and the printable-filter guard; (4) Any examples showing textarea usage reflect the current implementation.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Every file in docs/ is accurate for the current codebase. `grep -rn 'isWheel\|msg\.action.*press\|msg\.shift\|msg\.alt\|msg\.ctrl' docs/` returns zero hits. Key event handling is documented correctly.
sequentialTasks.completedNote: Scanned all 47 docs/ files. Active docs v2-clean. v1 patterns only in archived design spec (correctly marked). Added key resolution pattern with printable-filter guard to docs/scripting.md. lipgloss.newStyle() is our JS wrapper (not v1). lipgloss.NewStyle() is valid in v2.

sequentialTasks.description: Run `git diff main...HEAD` and review every changed file. Verify: (1) every change is intentional and tested; (2) no dead code introduced by the merge; (3) no v1 patterns survive; (4) all test helpers, golden files, and test data are current. Pay special attention to files that had merge conflicts (listed in WIP.md).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Complete diff audit documented in scratch/. Every changed file reviewed. No dead code. No untested changes. No v1 patterns. Merge conflict files specifically verified.
sequentialTasks.completedNote: Full audit documented in scratch/diff-vs-main-audit.md. Zero v1 patterns in production Go code. Fixed 3 stale comments (tea.KeyMsg → tea.KeyPressMsg). lipgloss.NewStyle() confirmed valid v2. deadcode linter clean. Zero shims. All changes tested.

sequentialTasks.task: Adversarial codebase autopsy on merged state
sequentialTasks.description: Spawn subagent with adversarial-codebase-autopsy skill to verify the merged codebase. The subagent reads the skill, performs exhaustive analysis, and produces a structured report. All issues found must be addressed before proceeding. This is a standing quality gate — the merged codebase must survive hostile scrutiny.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Autopsy report produced in scratch/. All identified issues addressed or documented with explicit rationale. No critical or high-severity findings remain open.
sequentialTasks.completedNote: Autopsy found 1 CRITICAL issue: v1 backward-compat shim in jsModToKeyMod() accepting boolean modifier properties. Shim deleted, benchmarks updated to v2 format, comprehensive test added verifying v1 booleans are rejected. All findings addressed.

sequentialTasks.description: Expose cursor position/shape/color/blink via view() return object. Currently view() returns {content, altScreen, mouseMode}. Add cursor field matching bubbletea v2's tea.View cursor capabilities. Files: internal/builtin/bubbletea/bubbletea.go (view handling), internal/command/super_document_script.js (view() return).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: JS controls cursor via view(). Integration test verifies cursor properties are set and applied.
sequentialTasks.completedNote: parseCursorProp() parses {x, y, shape, blink, color} from JS view object. Shape: block/underline/bar (case-insensitive). Blink defaults to true (tea.NewCursor default). Color via lipgloss.Color (hex/ANSI256). Cached for throttled renders. 14 subtests in TestParseCursorProp.

sequentialTasks.description: Expose terminal-wide foreground/background colors via view() return. Files: internal/builtin/bubbletea/bubbletea.go.

sequentialTasks.completedNote: foregroundColor and backgroundColor parsed as string props, converted via lipgloss.Color() supporting hex (#rrggbb, #rgb) and ANSI 256 index strings. Cached for throttled renders. 5 subtests in TestParseColorValue.

sequentialTasks.task: Scope expansion: identify and add next frontier tasks
sequentialTasks.description: After reaching stable-state (all current tasks complete, verified), ideate new improvements, refinements, and features. This task is never truly complete — it represents the perpetual expansion mandate. Areas to explore: additional textarea features (selection, cut/copy/paste), more comprehensive E2E test coverage, performance optimization, accessibility, new script examples, enhanced error messages, etc.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: New concrete tasks added to sequentialTasks. Each task meets the Planning Depth Standard (project-specific, verifiable acceptance, context pointers).

continuousVerification.task: Run build and test after EVERY significant change
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY test fails, STOP IMMEDIATELY and fix before proceeding.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: ZERO COMPATIBILITY SHIMS — v2 type-based dispatch ONLY. msg.mod array. msg.x/msg.y coordinates.
ALL platforms must pass. Cross-platform testing mandatory.

Key.Text is empty ('') for special keys (Enter, Tab, Esc, arrows) per ultraviolet decoder and bubbletea v2 docs. The msg.text resolution line is a defensive guard, not a bug fix. Never assume Key.Text contains the rune value — it is populated only for printable characters.
